programmes.show_description: The Scottish Conservative leader Russell Findlay speaks to Martin Geissler ahead of May's Holyrood election.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
News from across Scotland and the world, plus sport, weather, entertainment and big name interviews.
Stephen Jardine gets to the heart of the stories making the news.
News updates from Scotland and beyond, plus all your lunchtime sport, travel and weather.
Des Clarke hosts Scotland's award-winning satirical news quiz.
Nicola Meighan brings you feel-good music and chat with celebrity guests.
Breaking down the day's talking points with comment and opinion, plus sport, travel and weather.
Former fontman of Fine Young Cannibals Roland Gift shares a favourite vinyl record. The top 10 albums this week in 1999, and Grant pays tribute to Ian Dury and the Blockheads.
The final of BBC Introducing's Scottish Act of the Year. Listen in to hear the incredible performances, the judge's deliberation, and the artist who takes the title for 2026.
The best company to end the day, with lively chat and some of your favourite tunes.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
Mark Stephen and Rachel Stewart with stories from the great outdoors.
The stories that matter across Scotland and the world.
Amy Irons and Steven Mill kick-start your Saturday with lots of laughs, great guests and timeless tunes as we look back on the week's news and preview the day's football fixtures.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
Live commentary of St Johnstone v Queen's Park in the Scottish Championship.
Live commentary of St Johnstone v Queen's Park in the Scottish Championship.
Live commentary of Scotland v Japan from Hampden, as Steve Clarke's side prepare for the World Cup.
For the very best in traditional music, join Gary Innes for Take the Floor. We have not one, but two very special sessions from the archives to share with you.
Roddy Hart with a mix of classic tracks and new discoveries.
